pattern: The total of absolute deviations from a point is minimized when that point is the median, so sort, take the median, and sum each element's distance to it.
hints: Each move changes one element by one, so the cost to bring every element to some target T is the sum of |nums[i] - T|. You want the T that minimizes this sum.
The sum of absolute differences is minimized at the median of the values, not the mean. Sorting reveals the median directly.
Sort nums, take the middle element as the target, and return the sum of absolute differences from it. With an even count, any value between the two middle elements works and gives the same total.

edge_inputs.description: three consecutive values, median is the middle
even count, any value between the two middles is optimal
negatives included, median may be negative

stress.description: large arrays of random values including negatives
stress.note: exercises the linear-after-sort approach against the brute oracle

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def minMoves2(self, nums):
        # Pair the smallest with the largest inward; each pair must close its gap
        arr = sorted(nums)
        lo, hi = 0, len(arr) - 1
        total = 0
        while lo < hi:
            total += arr[hi] - arr[lo]
            lo += 1
            hi -= 1
        return total
